# CalSim View — Implementation Reference

> **Maintenance rule**: whenever a module is added, renamed, or restructured;
> a new API endpoint or schema field is added; the data pipeline or file layout
> changes; or a major frontend component is added or removed — **update this
> file in the same commit/session as the code change**. The goal is a document
> that always accurately reflects the running system.

## 1. Repository layout

```
calsim-view/
│
├── .github/memory/IMPLEMENTATION.md  ← this file
│
├── csview/                    ← Python package (backend + CLI tools)
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── geo/                   ← GeoSchematic loader + data models
│   │   ├── __init__.py
│   │   ├── models.py          ← GeoNode, GeoArc, GeoNetwork, type helpers
│   │   ├── loader.py          ← load_from_catalog(), load_from_dir(), _build_geojson()
│   │   ├── wresl_parser.py    ← enriches arcs with kind/units from WRESL files
│   │   └── __main__.py        ← CLI: build catalog.json + network.geojson from raw GeoJSON
│   │
   ├── study/                 ← Parquet-backed study results + DSS reader
│   │   ├── __init__.py
│   │   ├── store.py           ← StudyStore (lazy Parquet load, timeseries queries)
│   │   ├── dss_reader.py      ← HEC-DSS binary reader (merged from dss/)
│   │   └── __main__.py        ← CLI: DSS → results.parquet + results_meta.json
│   │
│   └── app/                   ← FastAPI application
│       ├── __init__.py
│       ├── __main__.py        ← CLI entry point (uvicorn runner)
│       ├── server.py          ← create_app() factory, lifespan, router mounting
│       ├── state.py           ← AppState singleton (network + studies)
│       ├── schemas.py         ← Pydantic response models
│       ├── routers/
│       │   ├── __init__.py
│       │   ├── network.py     ← /api/network endpoints
│       │   └── study.py       ← /api/study endpoints
│       ├── static/            ← built React app (generated by npm run build)
│       └── frontend/          ← React source
│           ├── package.json
│           ├── vite.config.js
│           ├── index.html
│           └── src/
│               ├── main.jsx
│               ├── App.jsx                ← root layout, drag-resize, study selector
│               ├── index.css
│               ├── api/
│               │   └── client.js          ← fetch wrappers for all API endpoints
│               └── components/
│                   ├── NetworkMap.jsx     ← Leaflet map, GeoJSON rendering, arrowheads
│                   ├── NodePanel.jsx      ← feature detail + timeseries chart
│                   ├── NeighborhoodGraph.jsx ← force-directed subgraph
│                   ├── NodeSearch.jsx     ← feature search autocomplete
│                   ├── StudySelector.jsx  ← active study dropdown
│                   └── ErrorBoundary.jsx
│
├── data/                      ← tracked build artifacts
│   ├── network/               ← pre-built network artifacts
│   │   ├── catalog.json       ← node/arc metadata + variable_to_node index
│   │   ├── network.geojson    ← GeoJSON FeatureCollection (4138 features)
│   │   ├── watersheds.geojson
│   │   ├── water_budget_areas.geojson
│   │   └── demand_units.geojson
│   └── study/                 ← compiled study artifacts (one subdir per study)
│       └── study_a/           ← opaque slug; logical name in results_meta.json
│           ├── results.parquet    ← pre-built wide DataFrame (1212 × 2312)
│           └── results_meta.json  ← study name, variable metadata, date range
│
└── reference/                 ← local-only, gitignored
    ├── geoschematic/          ← source GeoJSON (original: CalSim3_GeoSchematic_20221227)
    │   ├── i12_CalSim3Model_Arcs_20221021.geojson
    │   ├── i12_CalSim3Model_Nodes_20221021.geojson
    │   ├── README.md          ← notes original provenance
    │   └── ...                ← 7 GeoJSON files + README
    └── calsim-studies/
        └── study_a/           ← raw CalSim study data (repeat pattern for more)
            ├── DSS/           ← HEC-DSS binary files (large)
            ├── Run/           ← WRESL scripts + model tables
            └── study_meta.json ← study identity + provenance (name, description, …)

                                 Study identity is determined from results_meta.json
                                 (``study`` field), not the directory slug.

```

---

## 2. Architecture overview

```
 ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
 │                      One-time build steps                         │
 │                                                                   │
 │  Raw GeoSchematic GeoJSON  ──►  python -m csview.geo  ──►  data/network/
 │  (reference/geoschematic/)  catalog.json
 │                                      network.geojson
 │                                      overlay geojsons
 │
│  DSS output files  ──────────►  python -m csview.study  ──►  data/study/<slug>/
│  (reference/calsim-studies/)         results.parquet
 │                                      results_meta.json
 │
 │  React sources  ─────────────►  npm run build  ──────────►  csview/app/static/
 └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

 ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
 │                      Runtime                                        │
 │                                                                     │
 │  python -m csview.app --network-dir data/network/ --study data/study/study_a
 │       │                                                             │
 │       ▼                                                             │
 │   AppState.load()                                                   │
 │     ├── load_from_catalog(data/network/)    →  GeoNetwork in memory      │
 │     └── StudyStore.from_dir(data/study/study_a/)   →  lazy Parquet (loaded on   │
 │                                           first request)           │
 │                                                                     │
 │   FastAPI  ──►  /api/network/*  ──►  network router                │
 │           ──►  /api/study/*  ──►  study router                   │
 │           ──►  /*              ──►  React SPA (static files)       │
 └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

Key design decisions:

- **Single identifier per feature**: `feature_id` is the canonical key for
  both nodes (`cs3_id`, e.g. `"SHSTA"`) and arcs (`arc_id`, e.g. `"C_FOLSM"`).
  All API endpoints and frontend components use this field.
- **GeoJSON baked at build time**: `network.geojson` is written once at build
  time and served as a static file by the network router. It is not
  regenerated on each request.
- **Lazy Parquet loading**: `StudyStore` loads `results.parquet` into memory on
  the first `/api/study/feature/…` request; subsequent queries are in-memory
  column lookups.
- **Frontend built to static/**: The React app is compiled by Vite into
  `csview/app/static/`. FastAPI serves it with `StaticFiles(html=True)`, which
  handles all non-API routes as `index.html`.

---

## 3. Data pipeline — one-time builds

### 3a. Build the network catalog

```bash
python -m csview.geo \
    --geo-dir  reference/geoschematic \
    --wresl    reference/calsim-studies/study_a/Run/System \
    --out      data/network/
```

Reads raw GeoSchematic GeoJSON files from `reference/geoschematic/`
(gitignored, local only), optionally enriches arc `kind`/`units` from WRESL files, then writes:

- `data/network/catalog.json` — serialised node/arc metadata + `variable_to_node`
  mapping (used to resolve DSS variable names → GeoSchematic features)
- `data/network/network.geojson` — GeoJSON `FeatureCollection` with all arcs
  (LineStrings) and nodes (Points) as features with `feature_kind`,
  `arc_type`/`node_type` etc.
- Overlay files: `watersheds.geojson`, `water_budget_areas.geojson`,
  `demand_units.geojson`

### 3b. Build study results

```bash
python -m csview.study \
    --source  reference/calsim-studies/study_a \
    --catalog data/network/catalog.json \
    --out     data/study/study_a/
```

`--source` is the raw study directory in `reference/calsim-studies/` (gitignored,
local only — contains DSS output files, WRESL scripts, and `study_meta.json`). `--out` is the
compiled artifact destination in `data/study/` (tracked in git).

If `study_meta.json` is present in `--source`, the builder reads `name` and `description` from
it and writes them into `results_meta.json`. Schema:

```jsonc
// reference/calsim-studies/<slug>/study_meta.json
{
  "name": "danube_hist",          // logical name used by the app
  "description": "...",           // human-readable (copied into results_meta.json)
  "model_version": "9.3.1",
  "scenario": "Danube_Hist_v1.7",
  "dv_file":    "DSS/output/DCR2023_DV_9.3.1_Danube_Hist_v1.7.dss",  // DV DSS (relative to study dir)
  "gwout_file": "DSS/output/CVGroundwaterOut.dss",                    // GWOUT DSS (optional)
  "simulation_period": {"start": "1920-10", "end": "2021-09"},        // clips Parquet rows (YYYY-MM)
  "notes": "..."
}
```

`dv_file` and `gwout_file` paths are relative to the source directory and override
auto-discovery. If `dv_file` is absent the builder searches `DSS/output/` for a
file with `_DV_` in the name. If `gwout_file` is absent no groundwater output is
merged. `simulation_period` clips the combined DataFrame before writing Parquet
(rows outside the window are discarded; useful to exclude model spin-up).

Reads the DV DSS file (and GWOUT file when specified) from `<source>/DSS/output/`, resolves variable
names against `catalog.json`, then writes:

- `results.parquet` — wide Pandas DataFrame: rows = monthly dates
  (DatetimeIndex), columns = CalSim variable names (e.g. `C_FOLSM`, `S_SHSTA`)
- `results_meta.json` — `{study, built_at, dv_file, gwout_file, simulation_period, date_range, variables, node_dss_variables}`

The builder also optionally back-patches `catalog.json` to fill in
`node.dss_variables` lists and the `variable_to_node` mapping.

The study name displayed in the app is taken from `results_meta.json`
(`study` field), which is sourced from `study_meta.json` → fallback: source dir name.

### 3c. Build the frontend

```bash
cd csview/app/frontend
npm install
npm run build
```

Compiles the React/Vite source into `csview/app/static/`. The server
serves this directory as the SPA root.

---

## 4. Disk artifact reference

### `data/network/catalog.json`

```jsonc
{
  "nodes": {
    "SHSTA": {
      "cs3_id": "SHSTA",
      "description": "storage- reservoir or lake",
      "node_type": "Reservoir",     // derived by node_type_from_description()
      "lon": -122.41, "lat": 40.72,
      "hydro_region": "SAC",
      "river_name": "...", "nearest_gage": "...",
      "river_mile": 12.5,           // Optional[float]
      "c2vsim_gw": "...", "c2vsim_sw": "...", "calsim2_id": "...",
      "dss_variables": ["S_SHSTA", "E_SHSTA"]  // filled by results builder
    }
  },
  "arcs": {
    "C_FOLSM": {
      "arc_id": "C_FOLSM",
      "name": "American River at Folsom",
      "arc_type": "Channel",        // normalised via ARC_TYPE_MAP
      "sub_type": "Stream",
      "from_node": "FOLSM", "to_node": "AMD004",
      "hydro_region": "SAC",
      "description": "...", "units": "CFS", "kind": "CHANNEL",
      "coordinates": [[lon, lat], ...]
    }
  },
  "variable_to_node": {
    "S_SHSTA": "SHSTA",   // DSS variable name → GeoSchematic cs3_id
    "C_FOLSM": "C_FOLSM"  // arc variables map to themselves
  }
}
```

### `data/network/network.geojson`

GeoJSON `FeatureCollection` with **4 138 features** (2 602 arcs + 1 536 nodes).

Arc features:
```jsonc
{
  "type": "Feature",
  "geometry": {"type": "LineString", "coordinates": [...]},
  "properties": {
    "feature_id": "C_FOLSM",   // = arc_id.upper()
    "feature_kind": "arc",     // always "arc" for LineStrings
    "arc_type": "Channel",
    "name": "American River at Folsom",
    "sub_type": "Stream",
    "from_node": "FOLSM",
    "to_node": "AMD004",
    "hydro_region": "SAC",
    "description": "...",
    "units": "CFS",
    "kind": "CHANNEL"
  }
}
```

Node features:
```jsonc
{
  "type": "Feature",
  "geometry": {"type": "Point", "coordinates": [lon, lat]},
  "properties": {
    "feature_id": "SHSTA",     // = cs3_id.upper()
    "feature_kind": "node",    // always "node" for Points
    "node_type": "Reservoir",
    "description": "storage- reservoir or lake",
    "river_name": "...",
    "hydro_region": "SAC",
    ...
  }
}
```

> **Critical invariant**: `feature_kind` must be exactly `"arc"` or `"node"`.
> The frontend's map styling and neighborhood graph both branch on this value.
> `arc_type` and `node_type` are separate properties.

### `data/study/<slug>/results.parquet`

Wide Pandas DataFrame:
- Index: `DatetimeIndex`, monthly frequency, e.g. 1920-11-01 → 2021-10-01 (1212 rows)
- Columns: CalSim variable names (2312 columns), e.g. `C_FOLSM`, `S_SHSTA`
- Values: float64, units per variable (CFS or TAF)

### `data/study/<slug>/results_meta.json`

```jsonc
{
  "study": "danube_hist",   // logical name from study_meta.json in source dir
  "built_at": "2026-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  "dv_file": "...",
  "gwout_file": "...",       // null if not used
  "simulation_period": {"start": "1920-10", "end": "2021-09"},
  "date_range": {"start": "1920-11-01", "end": "2021-10-01"},
  "variables": {
    "C_FOLSM": {"feature_id": "C_FOLSM", "feature_kind": "arc", "units": "CFS", "kind": "CHANNEL", "c_part": "CHANNEL"},
    "S_SHSTA": {"feature_id": "SHSTA",   "feature_kind": "node", "units": "TAF", "kind": "STORAGE", "c_part": "STORAGE"}
  },
  "node_dss_variables": {"SHSTA": ["S_SHSTA", "E_SHSTA"]}
}
```

---

## 5. Python package — `csview`

### `csview.geo.models`

Core data classes and type-inference helpers.

| Symbol | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| `GeoNode` | Dataclass for a CalSim 3 point node (cs3_id, lat/lon, node_type, …) |
| `GeoArc`  | Dataclass for an arc (arc_id, from_node, to_node, arc_type, coordinates, …) |
| `GeoNetwork` | Container: `nodes: Dict[str, GeoNode]`, `arcs: Dict[str, GeoArc]`, `geojson: dict`, `variable_to_node: Dict[str, str]`, plus helper methods `lookup_node()`, `lookup_arc()` |
| `node_type_from_description(desc)` | Derives `node_type` category from NodeDescription text. Checks `"groundwater"` before `"storage"` to avoid misclassifying GW-demand nodes. |
| `ARC_TYPE_MAP` | Dict normalising raw GeoSchematic Type strings → canonical arc type label (e.g. `"Diverison"` → `"Diversion"`) |
| `KIND_TO_UNITS` | WRESL kind → default units string |
| `ARC_TYPE_TO_UNITS` | Arc type → typical units (fallback) |
| `GeoNetwork.feature_types()` | Returns `Dict[str, List[str]]` with keys `"node_types"` and `"arc_types"` — used by `/api/network/types` and `/api/network/arc-types` |

### `csview.geo.loader`

| Function | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| `load_from_catalog(catalog_dir)` | **Runtime path**. Reads `catalog.json` + `network.geojson` from disk → `GeoNetwork`. Called by `AppState.load()` at startup. |
| `load_from_dir(geo_dir, wresl_dir)` | **Build-time path**. Parses raw GeoSchematic GeoJSON files, optionally enriches from WRESL, calls `_build_geojson`. |
| `_build_geojson(gn)` | Builds `gn.geojson` dict from `gn.nodes` and `gn.arcs`. Sets `feature_kind="arc"` + `arc_type` for LineStrings; `feature_kind="node"` + `node_type` for Points. |

> Zone nodes (`"02"`, `"03"`) appear in arc `from_node` fields in the source
> data but have no point geometry and therefore no `GeoNode` entry. The
> neighborhood BFS (`get_neighborhood` in network.py) filters these out via
> `rendered_node_ids` to prevent phantom nodes with no position.

### `csview.study.store`

`StudyStore` — primary interface for result data.

| Property / method | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `.name` | Study name (logical, read from `results_meta.json` `study` field) |
| `.variables` | List of all variable names (reads Parquet schema header, no full load) |
| `.date_range` | `(start_str, end_str)` — reads `date_start`/`date_end` from `results_meta.json`; falls back to Parquet index only if those fields are absent |
| `.get_series(varname)` | Returns a `pd.Series` for one variable (triggers full Parquet load on first call) |
| `.get_feature_series(feature_id, node_map)` | Returns all series whose variable maps to `feature_id` |
| `StudyStore.from_dir(study_dir)` | Constructor — validates `results.parquet` exists |

### `csview.study.__main__` (CLI builder)

> **Performance — IMPORTANT**: Reading both DSS files takes **3–5 minutes**.
> Always use `run_in_terminal` with `isBackground=true` and a log file, then
> call `await_terminal` with timeout ≥ 360 000 ms before reading results.
> Do **not** run it blocking/foreground and do **not** assume it has finished
> until `results_meta.json` shows a new `built_at` timestamp.
>
> ```bat
> C:\Users\warnold\Miniconda3\envs\py38\python.exe -m csview.study ^
>     --source reference/calsim-studies/study_a ^
>     --catalog data/network/catalog.json ^
>     --out data/study/study_a/ > build_log.txt 2>&1
> ```
>
> After `await_terminal`, verify with `type build_log.txt`.

Variable resolution strategy:

1. If `varname.upper()` matches an `arc_id` directly → feature is that arc.
2. If `varname.upper()` matches `<PREFIX><cs3_id>` (e.g. `GP_07N_NU`,
   `S_SHSTA`) → feature is `cs3_id`. Prefixes are tried longest-first to
   prevent partial stripping (e.g. `GP_` before `G_`).

   Recognised prefixes and their CalSim 3 semantics:

   | Prefix | Meaning |
   |--------|---------|
   | `XNM_TOTAL_` | Total non-market exchange |
   | `PEAKAW_` | Peak agricultural water demand |
   | `SUMAW_` | Sum of agricultural water deliveries |
   | `SUMUD_` | Sum of urban demand |
   | `SHRTG_` | Shortage |
   | `C2V_` | C2VSim-linked demand node |
   | `CVP_` | Central Valley Project delivery |
   | `SWP_` | State Water Project delivery |
   | `WTS_` | Water transfer / wheeling supply |
   | `AW_` | Agricultural water (applied) |
   | `DG_` | Demand — groundwater component |
   | `DL_` | Demand — delivery level |
   | `DN_` | Demand — native surface water |
   | `DP_` | Demand — delivery percent |
   | `EG_` | Estimated groundwater |
   | `EL_` | Evapotranspiration (local) |
   | `EV_` | Evapotranspiration |
   | `GP_` | Groundwater pumping |
   | `LF_` | Local flows / local surface water |
   | `MF_` | Minimum flow requirement |
   | `OS_` | Outflow / seepage |
   | `RP_` | Riparian recovery / return flow |
   | `RU_` | Return flow — urban |
   | `SL_` | Seepage loss |
   | `A_` | Applied water |
   | `C_` | Carryover |
   | `D_` | Delivery / demand |
   | `E_` | Exogenous |
   | `F_` | Flow (misc.) |
   | `G_` | Groundwater (generic) |
   | `R_` | Return flow |
   | `S_` | Storage |
   | `T_` | Tributary |
   | `X_` | Exchange |

### `csview.app.state`

`AppState` singleton (`state` module-level instance in `state.py`).

| Attribute | Type | Content |
|---|---|---|
| `.network` | `GeoNetwork \| None` | Loaded GeoNetwork |
| `.studies` | `Dict[str, StudyStore]` | All registered studies |
| `.active_study` | `str \| None` | Default study name |

`get_state()` generator is the FastAPI dependency used by all routers.

### `csview.app.schemas`

Pydantic response models:

| Model | Used for |
|---|---|
| `FeatureSummary` | `/api/network/features` list items |
| `NodeDetail` | `/api/network/features/{id}` when feature is a node |
| `ArcDetail` | `/api/network/features/{id}` when feature is an arc |
| `NeighborhoodResponse` | `/api/network/features/{id}/neighborhood` |
| `NeighborhoodNode` / `NeighborhoodArc` | Items within neighborhood response |
| `StudyInfo` | Items in `/api/study/studies` |
| `StudyListResponse` | `/api/study/studies` |
| `FeatureResultSeries` | `/api/study/feature/{id}` and `/api/study/variable/{prmname}` |

---

## 6. Backend — FastAPI application

### Startup sequence

1. `python -m csview.app` → `main()` in `__main__.py` → calls `create_app()`
2. `create_app()` registers routers + `lifespan` context
3. On lifespan entry: `AppState.load(network_dir, study_paths)` is called
   - Loads `catalog.json` + `network.geojson` → `GeoNetwork` in memory
   - For each study path: constructs `StudyStore` (does **not** load Parquet yet)
4. Uvicorn begins serving requests

### Router summary

**`/api/network`** (routers/network.py)

| Method | Path | Returns |
|---|---|---|
| GET | `/api/network` | Full `network.geojson` FeatureCollection |
| GET | `/api/network/features` | `List[FeatureSummary]` (filterable) |
| GET | `/api/network/features/{feature_id}` | `NodeDetail` or `ArcDetail` |
| GET | `/api/network/features/{feature_id}/neighborhood` | `NeighborhoodResponse` |
| GET | `/api/network/regions` | `List[str]` — distinct hydro regions |
| GET | `/api/network/types` | `List[str]` — distinct node types |
| GET | `/api/network/arc-types` | `List[str]` — distinct arc types |

**`/api/study`** (routers/study.py)

| Method | Path | Returns |
|---|---|---|
| GET | `/api/study/studies` | `StudyListResponse` |
| POST | `/api/study/studies/{name}/activate` | `{active: name}` |
| GET | `/api/study/studies/{name}/variables` | `List[str]` |
| GET | `/api/study/feature/{feature_id}` | `FeatureResultSeries` |
| GET | `/api/study/variable/{prmname}` | `FeatureResultSeries` |

**`/api/network/overlays/{layer}`** — `layer` ∈ `watersheds`, `water_budget`, `demand_unit`.

**`/`** — React SPA served from `csview/app/static/` via `StaticFiles(html=True)`.

### Neighborhood BFS algorithm

`get_neighborhood(feature_id, depth)` in `routers/network.py`:

1. Start: look up seed feature. If node → `visited_nodes = {cs3_id}`;
   if arc → add both `from_node` and `to_node`.
2. BFS to `depth` hops: for each node in the frontier, find all arcs whose
   `from_node` or `to_node` matches; add the other endpoint to the next frontier.
3. `rendered_node_ids` = subset of `visited_nodes` where `gn.lookup_node(nid)
   is not None` — filters out phantom zone nodes (`"02"`, `"03"`) which have
   no GeoNode entry and no geometry.
4. Output arcs: only arcs where *both* `from_node` and `to_node` are in
   `rendered_node_ids` (prevents arcs from dangling off phantom nodes).

---

## 7. REST API reference

### GET /api/network

Returns the full `network.geojson` as loaded from disk. No query params.

### GET /api/network/features

Query params: `feature_kind`, `hydro_region`, `node_type`, `arc_type` (all optional).

### GET /api/network/features/{feature_id}

`feature_id` is case-insensitive (normalised to upper). Returns `NodeDetail`
if the id matches a `cs3_id`, else `ArcDetail` if it matches an `arc_id`,
else 404.

### GET /api/network/features/{feature_id}/neighborhood

Query param: `depth` (int, default 2). Returns `NeighborhoodResponse` with
`nodes` and `arcs` arrays. Arc IDs in the response are `arc.arc_id` values,
node `feature_id`s are `cs3_id` values.

### GET /api/study/feature/{feature_id}

Query param: `study` (optional, defaults to active study). Returns `FeatureResultSeries`:

```jsonc
{
  "feature_id": "SHSTA",
  "study": "danube_hist",
  "series": {
    "S_SHSTA": [["1921-09-30", 4552.1], ...],
    "E_SHSTA": [...]
  },
  "metadata": {
    "S_SHSTA": {"units": "TAF", "kind": "STORAGE", "c_part": "STORAGE"},
    "E_SHSTA": {"units": "TAF", "kind": "EVAPORATION", "c_part": "EVAPORATION"}
  }
}
```

Returns 404 if no variables map to `feature_id` in the active study.

**Node features only** — the response is enriched with the series of every arc
that connects to the requested node. Each injected arc entry carries an extra
`"direction"` field in `metadata`:

- `"in"` — arc's `to_node` equals the requested node (flow arriving)
- `"out"` — arc's `from_node` equals the requested node (flow leaving)

Arcs that have no matching series in the active study are silently omitted.

### GET /api/study/variable/{prmname}

Query param: `study` (optional). Returns `FeatureResultSeries` for a single DSS variable.

### GET /api/network/overlays/{layer}

`layer` ∈ `watersheds`, `water_budget`, `demand_unit`. Returns the corresponding GeoJSON overlay.

---

## 8. Frontend — React / Leaflet SPA

**Stack**: React 18, Vite 6, Tailwind CSS 4, React-Leaflet 4, Leaflet 1.9,
leaflet-arrowheads 1.4, Recharts 2, TanStack Query 5.

### `App.jsx` — root layout

- Three-column resizable layout: `[NeighborhoodGraph] | [NodePanel] | [Map]`
- State: `selectedNode` (feature_id string), `flyToFeature`, `activeStudy`
- Drag-resize handles between columns (mouse + touch)
- `handleFeatureClick(featureId)` — sets selected node, opens panel
- `handleNeighborhoodNodeClick(featureId)` — selects node AND flies map to it

### `NetworkMap.jsx` — interactive map

- GeoJSON loaded once via React Query (`fetchNetwork`), cached indefinitely
- `GeoJSONLayer` component: mounted once, styles updated reactively via
  `layer.setStyle()` (no remount on selection change)
- **Arc styling**: `ARC_STYLE` table keyed by `arc_type`; unrecognised types
  fall back to `DEFAULT_ARC_STYLE` `{ color: "#4b5563", w: 1.0 }`
- **Node styling**: `NODE_STYLE` table keyed by `node_type`; circle markers
  via `pointToLayer`; unrecognised types → `DEFAULT_NODE_STYLE` gray dot
- **Flow direction**: `leaflet-arrowheads` adds a filled arrowhead at each
  arc's downstream end (`frequency: "endonly"`)
- **Hover delay on arcs**: default Leaflet mouseover is replaced with a 220 ms
  delayed `openTooltip()` so brief cursor crossings don't flash tooltips
- **Tooltip**: non-sticky, snaps to feature; shows `feature_id`, name/description, type
- **Selected feature**: yellow highlight (`#facc15`) + `bringToFront()`
- **Legend**: left-side overlay listing all node types and all arc types

#### Node type → colour mapping

| node_type | colour |
|---|---|
| Reservoir | `#3b82f6` blue |
| Groundwater | `#10b981` emerald |
| Groundwater Storage | `#059669` green (no nodes currently in GeoSchematic) |
| Demand-Agricultural | `#f59e0b` amber |
| Demand-Urban | `#f97316` orange |
| Demand-Refuge | `#84cc16` lime |
| Shortage | `#ef4444` red |
| Contract | `#eab308` yellow |
| Return Flow | `#a3e635` lime |
| Evaporation | `#475569` dark slate |
| Water Quality | `#22d3ee` cyan |
| Power | `#a855f7` purple |
| *(default)* | `#6b7280` gray |

#### Arc type → colour mapping

| arc_type | colour |
|---|---|
| Channel | `#60a5fa` light blue |
| Diversion | `#34d399` emerald |
| Inflow | `#2dd4bf` teal |
| Return Flow | `#a3e635` lime |
| Minimum Flow | `#38bdf8` sky |
| Surface Runoff | `#86efac` light green |
| Seepage | `#64748b` slate |
| Deep Percolation | `#6b7280` gray |
| Tile Drain | `#78716c` stone |
| Groundwater | `#059669` green |
| Evaporation | `#475569` dark slate |
| Spill | `#7dd3fc` pale sky |
| Closure Term | `#4b5563` gray |
| Delta Accretion | `#a78bfa` violet |
| Delta Depletion | `#f472b6` pink |
| *(default)* | `#4b5563` gray |

### `ResultsChart.jsx` — time series chart

Accepts `series` (dict of variable name → `[[date, value]]` rows),
`metadata` (dict of variable name → `{units, kind, ...}` from the API), and
`dateRange` (optional `[startYear, endYear]` strings).

#### Series splitting

`splitSeries(series, metadata)` partitions variables into three buckets:

| Bucket | Criteria | Chart type |
|---|---|---|
| `arcFlows` | `metadata[key].direction` is present (`"in"` or `"out"`) | `StackedFlowChart` |
| `storage` | `kind` ∈ `STORAGE`, `STORAGE-ZONE`, `GW-STORAGE`, `GROUNDWATER` | `LineChartPanel` |
| `balance` | everything else | `LineChartPanel` |

#### Sub-components

**`LineChartPanel`** — plain Recharts `LineChart`
- Always renders values as-is (no negation)
- Y-axis anchored to 0 (`yDomain` always includes 0)
- Dashed zero reference line
- Year boundary vertical lines
- Dashed average reference line
- CFS→TAF toggle applies when `units === "CFS"`

**`StackedFlowChart`** — Recharts `ComposedChart` with stacked areas
- Renders each variable as a stacked `Area` (fill) + `Line` (edge)
- `"out"` direction arcs have their values **negated** so they stack below zero
- Legend and tooltip show `(in)` / `(out)` suffix derived from `metadata[key].direction`
- Y-axis anchored to 0; dashed zero reference line
- CFS→TAF toggle applies

#### Main export layout (top to bottom)

1. **Arc Flows** — `StackedFlowChart` with CFS/TAF pill toggle (only when `arcFlows` is non-empty; node features only)
2. **Storage** — `LineChartPanel` (only when `storage` is non-empty)
3. **Water Balance / Flow** — `LineChartPanel` for `balance` variables (label is "Flow" for arc features, "Water Balance" for node features)

#### Shared helpers

| Helper | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `buildChartData(seriesMap, dateRange, negateKeys, convertFn)` | Merges series into Recharts row objects, optionally negating and converting units |
| `daysInMonth(dateKey)` | Returns days in a YYYY-MM-DD month for TAF conversion |
| `cfsToTaf(value, dateKey)` | Converts CFS → TAF using month length |
| `yDomain(rows, keys)` | Computes `[min, max]` always including 0 |
| `shortKey(key, keyLabels)` | Trims DSS-style prefix/suffix for legend display |
| `ToggleLegend` | Checkbox list to show/hide individual series |
| `ChartTooltip` | Formatted tooltip showing date + all active series values |

### `NeighborhoodGraph.jsx` — local subgraph

- Fetches `NeighborhoodResponse` via `fetchNeighborhood(featureId)`
- Custom D3-style force layout (`computeLayout`) positions nodes
- Renders SVG: nodes as circles coloured by `node_type`, arcs as lines
- `onNodeClick` callback navigates the main selection and flies the map

### `NodeSearch.jsx` — search bar

- Fetches all features via `fetchFeatures()` on mount
- Filters by typed query (case-insensitive substring on `feature_id` and `description`)
- Selecting a result calls `onSelect(feature_id)`

### `StudySelector.jsx`

- Renders a dropdown of available study names from `fetchStudies()`
- Active study change propagates up to `App` → passed into `NodePanel`

### `api/client.js`

All fetch calls go through the `get(path)` helper which throws on non-2xx.
Base path: `/api`.

| Export | Endpoint |
|---|---|
| `fetchNetwork()` | GET /api/network |
| `fetchFeatures(params)` | GET /api/network/features |
| `fetchFeature(id)` | GET /api/network/features/{id} |
| `fetchNeighborhood(id, depth)` | GET /api/network/features/{id}/neighborhood |
| `fetchRegions()` | GET /api/network/regions |
| `fetchNodeTypes()` | GET /api/network/types |
| `fetchArcTypes()` | GET /api/network/arc-types |
| `fetchStudies()` | GET /api/study/studies |
| `fetchFeatureResults(id, study)` | GET /api/study/feature/{id} |

---

## 9. Running the application

### First-time setup (if rebuildng artifacts)

```bash
# 1. Build the network catalog (only needed if re-processing the GeoSchematic)
#    All inputs are in reference/ (gitignored, local only).
python -m csview.geo \
    --geo-dir reference/geoschematic \
    --wresl   reference/calsim-studies/study_a/Run/System \
    --out     data/network/

# 2. Build results for a study (only needed if re-running a CalSim study)
#    --source is the raw study dir (in reference/, gitignored)
#    --out    is the compiled artifact dir (in data/study/, tracked in git)
python -m csview.study \
    --source  reference/calsim-studies/study_a \
    --catalog data/network/catalog.json \
    --out     data/study/study_a/

# 3. Build the frontend (re-run after any frontend source change)
cd csview/app/frontend
npm install
npm run build
cd ../../..
```

### Run the server

```bash
python -m csview.app \
    --network-dir data/network/ \
    --study data/study/study_a
```

Open `http://localhost:8000`.

Multiple studies:

```bash
python -m csview.app \
    --network-dir data/network/ \
    --study data/study/study_a \
    --study data/study/study_b \
    --default-study danube_hist
```

Options: `--host 0.0.0.0`, `--port 8080`, `--reload` (dev auto-reload),
`--verbose`.

### Current study: `danube_hist` (source slug: `study_a`, output slug: `study_a`)

- Results: 2312 variables × 1212 monthly time steps (1920-11 → 2021-10)
- Parquet: `data/study/study_a/results.parquet` (~15.5 MB)
- 2231 arc variables, 81 node variables

---

## 10. Environment and dependencies

### Python

- **Environment**: `C:\Users\warnold\Miniconda3\envs\py38` (Python 3.8)
- **Key packages**: `fastapi`, `uvicorn`, `pydantic`, `pandas`, `pyarrow`,
  `pydsstools` (DSS reader)

Install via `pip install -r requirements.txt` (or `pyproject.toml`).

### Node / npm

- **Node** 18+, **npm** 9+
- All frontend deps in `csview/app/frontend/package.json`
- Key libs: `leaflet@1.9`, `react-leaflet@4`, `leaflet-arrowheads@1.4`,
  `recharts@2`, `@tanstack/react-query@5`, `tailwindcss@4`, `vite@6`

*Last updated: 2026-06-09 (CFS/TAF unit toggle on lineplots; zero-line always visible (yDomain anchored to 0); GET /api/study/feature/{id} now enriches node responses with connected arc series including direction:"in"/"out" metadata; ResultsChart.jsx fully rewritten — splitSeries partitions into arcFlows/storage/balance, StackedFlowChart renders in/out arcs as stacked areas with outflows negated, LineChartPanel handles storage and water-balance; previous: renamed calsim→csview package; merged dss/ into study/dss_reader.py; renamed csview/results→csview/study; renamed /api/results→/api/study; moved network/ and study/ into data/; study subdirs use opaque slugs study_a/study_b; logical name from results_meta.json; fixed date_range to read metadata before Parquet; added missing endpoints: overlays, study activate/variables, variable series; moved GeoSchematic source to reference/geoschematic/; renamed calsim-studies/danube_hist→study_a with study_meta.json; builder reads study_meta.json for logical name/description; dss_file→dv_file + gwout_file in study_meta.json; simulation_period clips Parquet; GWOUT DSS merged into DV results; expanded _NODE_PREFIXES from 9 to 34 prefixes covering GP_, SHRTG_, DL_, DG_, EV_, AW_, RP_, RU_, etc. — longest first to prevent partial stripping)*
